text: Abstract Even though the research of machine learning (ML) algorithms has received a high degree of attention in the literature in the last few decades, many researchers (especially those that are not so familiar with the basic statistics concepts) do not know how to calculate performance metrics for various supervised learning algorithms such as: - binary, multi-class and multi-label classification - regression - image segmentation - object detection - information retrieval And how to choose the appropriate statistical test for comparing machine learning models or algorithms and what are the key parameters and values that need to be calculated and known to perform the appropriate statistical tests, how to obtain these metrics, how to apply the statistical test and the interpretation of its results, all of these and more examples will be demonstrated in this tutorial, specifically with the comparison and evaluation of some deep learning convolutional neural networks (CNNs) that are used to classify X-Ray images infected with different lung diseases, as well as detecting cancer tumors in positron emission tomography (PET) images.
explanation: The text has a generic, textbook-like tone with minor formatting irregularities (inconsistent capitalization and dash-list structure) that are common in AI-generated drafts; the topic and phrasing are broad and typical of synthetic writing.
